survivors_flagged.one_line: Live Cintas arb: $155 cash + 0.7720 CTAS/sh, holders approved Jun'11; pending H2'26 close w/ FTC Second Request — real dated spread, antitrust the variable.

Dated arrangement agreement to combine with Sweetwater Royalties ($1.9B, Apr-2026) + Nasdaq re-listing; structural transformation pending close, though UROY is the surviving vehicle (not a clean spread).
Dated milestones: Q3-2026 first separated-oxide production + $1.6B Commerce funding finalized + China export-control overhang; supply/timing-moat but largely thematic ramp, not classic special-sit.
Definitive de-SPAC merger with NorthStar Earth & Space (→NSTR, $300M val, $30M PIPE), outside date Jan-31-2027; dated combination but speculative microcap target.
Live merger-arb target: Transocean all-stock take-out ($5.8B, 15.235 RIG/VAL), DOJ Second Request adds antitrust optionality, 2H-2026 target close — a real dated complicated arb.
$4.4B all-stock merger into Axcelis; holders approved Feb-26, only China SAMR left, close 2H26 — clean arb with a regulatory gate.
Atacicept PDUFA July 7, 2026 (8 days out), priority review for IgA nephropathy — hard dated binary, first BAFF/APRIL B-cell modulator.
Asset-light restructuring offloads ~$6.9B debt to manufacturing arm, close expected Q3-26 pending creditor approvals — dated but founder-driven, low-float equity lottery.
Phase 3 ECLIPSE-1 hep-delta topline expected Q4-26; real binary but ~2 quarters out with soft 'expected' timing.
VANQUISH-1/2 Phase 3 obesity fully enrolled; maintenance-dosing data 3Q26 + oral Ph3 start 4Q26 — one real dated forward catalyst but pivotal topline is the harder event and crowded GLP-1 thesis.
Bysanti (Feb 2026) + NEREUS/tradipitant already approved/fired; forward dated binary = imsidolimab GPP PDUFA Dec 12 2026 — one real dated FDA decision.
Live sale process disclosed Jun 23 2026 — Thoma Bravo/Blackstone/Vista circling, advisers engaged, preliminary not signed; forward mispriced cyber-security take-out, NOT fired.
Povetacicept IgAN PDUFA Nov 30 2026 is a real dated binary, but on a ~$100B mega-cap the asymmetry is thin; pipeline-diversification, not mispriced event.
Live Paramount Skydance $30→$31 all-cash tender, board deems superior; DOJ Second Request cleared, $0.25/qtr ticking-fee past Dec-31-2026. Complicated arb.
Signed sale to Banco Santander ($48.75 cash + 2.0548 ADS, ~$12.3B); OCC approved Jun-12-2026, Fed+ECB pending, 2H-2026 close. Complicated cash-and-stock cross-border arb.

verified.catalyst: Cintas's signed, shareholder-approved acquisition of UniFirst ($155 cash + 0.7720 CTAS/sh) closing if/when the FTC clears it past the June 11, 2026 Second Request.
verified.dated_milestone: FTC Second Request received Jun 11, 2026; HSR clock runs to 30 days after substantial compliance. Next: expected close H2 2026; contractual outside date Jan 10, 2027 (extendable to ~Sep 2027).
verified.instrument: UNF common (merger-arb long; spread vs $155 cash + 0.7720 CTAS)
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty Cintas/CTAS), G2 (concrete signed + 99%-approved merger agreement, fixed consideration), G3 (specific unpriced figure: the ~8% gross spread the tape discounts for antitrust risk)
verified.edge: L — spread is wide because FTC Second Request on a #1-buys-#3 horizontal combo is genuine block/divestiture risk; ~45% of consideration floats with CTAS (down ~20% since signing). Even-money, not asymmetric.
verified.primary_source: UniFirst 8-K / Cintas 8-K via SEC + StockTitan/TradingView (FTC Second Request Jun 11, 2026; shareholder approval Jun 12, 2026; close expected H2 2026); live FMP quotes UNF $263.94 / CTAS $169.08 (2026-06-29)
verified.analysis: Verified live and unfired: UNF $263.94 vs deal value $155 + 0.7720×CTAS $169.08 = $285.53, a ~+8% gross spread; shareholders approved 99%+ on Jun 12, 2026 (no vote risk), FTC Second Request Jun 11, 2026, close guided to H2 2026, outside date Jan 10, 2027 extendable to ~Sep 2027. This is a real, dated, forward merger-arb (clears all three Bloom gates), but the edge is low: the spread reflects authentic antitrust tail risk on a top-tier horizontal combination, and a break reverts UNF toward a ~$215 standalone floor (-19%) against only ~+8% upside, so the asymmetry is roughly even-money rather than the 3.8:1 the board's stale floor implied. No FTC resolution, divestiture signal, or block has occurred since the Jun 11 Second Request, so the catalyst remains pending and forward.

verified.catalyst: UROY shareholders vote July 20, 2026 on the dated Apr-2026 arrangement to combine with Orion's ~92% interest in Sweetwater Royalties (~US$1.9B EV / US$1.14B equity), with UROY rolling 1:1 into a new US-listed Nasdaq parent expected to close early Q3 2026.
verified.dated_milestone: Special shareholder meeting July 20, 2026 (66 2/3% vote); then court + Nasdaq listing + regulatory approval, close targeted early Q3 2026
verified.instrument: equity (UROY common; rolls 1:1 into New URC)
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty: Orion Resource Partners / Sweetwater Royalties LLC) + G2 (concrete committed terms: signed arrangement, US$330M cash + 223.25M shares, July 20 vote, 14.37% insider support)
verified.edge: M — real dated transformational event with insider support and a fixed vote date, and UROY trades US$2.82 vs the US$3.64 deal-reference share price (below 50/200d avgs, near 52w low); but it is NOT a clean arb spread (holders roll 1:1 into Newco rather than a cash takeout, so no fixed premium to capture) and carries deal/dilution + uranium-macro overlay.
verified.primary_source: UROY Form 6-K / management information circular and press release (Apr 16, 2026 announcement; meeting set for July 20, 2026), via SEC EDGAR / StockTitan / Globe and Mail
verified.analysis: Verified: a signed Apr-16-2026 plan of arrangement combines UROY with Orion's ~92% Sweetwater Royalties interest (~US$1.9B EV, US$1.14B equity) into a new US-domiciled Nasdaq-listed parent; the dated, forward catalyst is the July 20, 2026 special meeting (66 2/3% threshold, 14.37% insiders committed), with court/Nasdaq/regulatory clearances and close targeted early Q3 2026 — unfired. This is a structural transformation, not a clean spread: existing holders receive one New URC share per share (no cash takeout premium), so the thesis is a re-rating of a scaled, US-listed royalty vehicle currently priced at US$2.82 vs the deal's US$3.64 reference, not a deterministic arb capture. Gated to WATCH/6 because the milestone is concrete and dated but lacks a fixed capturable spread and the underlying carries uranium/royalty macro exposure plus dilution from US$330M cash + ~223M new shares.

verified.catalyst: First production of separated heavy rare earth oxides (Dy/Tb/Y) targeted at the just-commissioned Wheat Ridge demonstration plant in Q3-2026.
verified.dated_milestone: Q3-2026 first separated heavy-oxide production at demo plant; next: Round Top DFS completion Q4-2026 (publication Q1-2027), mine production late-2028
verified.bloom_gates: None pass cleanly. G1 FAIL (no named third-party offtake; only internal subsidiary Less Common Metals). G2: the $1.6B Commerce commitment is concrete but already FINALIZED 2026-06-03, so it has fired, not forward. G3 FAIL (Q3-2026 demo-oxide milestone has no committed volume or contracted dollar figure and is widely covered/priced).
verified.edge: L — the headline catalyst (the $1.6B Commerce funding) was finalized 2026-06-03 and the demo plant commissioned 2026-06-15, both public; stock is +113% YTD on "Strong Buy" with targets ~$37-39, so the thesis is consensus and re-rated, leaving no informational edge.
verified.primary_source: USA Rare Earth PR via StockTitan, "Commissions Hydrometallurgical Demonstration Facility, Targeting Heavy Rare Earth Oxide Production in Q3 2026" (2026-06-15); funding finalization GlobeNewswire/NIST CHIPS (2026-06-03); China export-control listing (2026-06-22).
verified.analysis: The two triage catalysts have substantively fired: the $1.6B Commerce/CHIPS funding moved from LOI (Jan-2026) to FINALIZED definitive agreements on 2026-06-03, and the hydromet demo plant was commissioned 2026-06-15. What remains forward is Q3-2026 first separated heavy-oxide production, but it is demonstration-scale (28 staff, no disclosed capacity), feeds an internal subsidiary with no named third-party offtake, and is a thematic supply-shortage ramp rather than a discrete special-sit re-rating event. The China export-control addition (2026-06-22) is a macro/geopolitical overhang, not a company-specific unpriced trigger; with the stock already up 113% YTD on consensus Strong-Buy coverage, no Bloom gate clears the strict forward/unpriced/named-counterparty bar.

verified.catalyst: Definitive de-SPAC business combination: Viking Acquisition Corp. I (VACI) to merge with NorthStar Earth & Space at a $300M pre-money valuation with a fully committed $30M PIPE anchored by Cartesian Capital, relisting as NSTR on NYSE.
verified.dated_milestone: Definitive BCA announced 2026-04-17; expected close Q3 2026 (next: SEC registration-statement effectiveness then shareholder vote, ~Q3 2026; outside date plausibly ~Jan-31-2027 per triage but NOT confirmable in reached primary sources)
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty: NorthStar Earth & Space) + G2 (concrete commitment: signed definitive business combination agreement + $30M fully committed PIPE anchored by Cartesian Capital); G3 weak — no specific currently-unpriced figure (shares at $10.22 ~ trust, no disclosed discount/spread).
verified.edge: L — pre-vote SPAC trading at $10.22 (~trust, $320M cap, 50d $10.21), so deal optionality is already priced and there is no discount-to-trust or merger-spread edge; target is a speculative pre-scale space SSA/SDA microcap with only a $30M PIPE, low conviction on post-deal re-rate.
verified.primary_source: GlobeNewswire / NorthStar Earth & Space press release "Announces Plans to Become Publicly Traded via Merger with Viking Acquisition Corp. I" (2026-04-17); corroborated by SEC Form 8-K/425 (CIK 2080023, filed 2026-04) and FMP quote VACI $10.22 (timestamp 2026-06).
verified.analysis: VACI is a genuine, dated, forward de-SPAC: a definitive business combination agreement (announced 2026-04-17) with named counterparty NorthStar Earth & Space, a $300M pre-money valuation, a fully committed $30M PIPE anchored by Cartesian Capital, and an expected Q3 2026 close relisting as NSTR. Flag corrections: the SPAC is Viking (not "Voyager") Acquisition Corp. I, and the Jan-31-2027 outside date could not be confirmed in the sources reached (SEC 8-K returned HTTP 403); the announced Q3-2026 close is consistent with it. It clears Bloom G1/G2 but the shares sit at trust value (~$10.22) with no merger spread or unpriced figure (G3) and a speculative early-stage microcap target, so the dated catalyst exists but offers low investable edge — WATCH, not ACTIVE.

verified.catalyst: Live all-stock take-out by Transocean (15.235 RIG per VAL, ~$5.8B) where DOJ Second Request antitrust review gates a 2H-2026 close.
verified.dated_milestone: Substantial compliance with the May 4, 2026 DOJ Second Request, which restarts a 30-day HSR clock; original close target 2H-2026. Next dated event: Q2 earnings / merger update mid-Aug 2026 (no compliance date disclosed yet).
verified.instrument: equity (VAL common; arb = long VAL / short 15.235x RIG)
verified.primary_source: Valaris 8-K (received DOJ Second Request May 4, 2026), filed May 5, 2026, via StockTitan; deal terms confirmed Offshore Energy / MergerSight; live VAL $74.53 / RIG $5.04 quotes 2026-06-29.
verified.kill_fact: Primary SEC 8-Ks confirm the core arb to the cent: Transocean's all-stock BCA (signed Feb-9-2026) pays 15.235 RIG/VAL (~$5.8B), and the DOJ issued a HSR Second Request on May-4-2026 with no substantial-compliance date disclosed, leaving the deal genuinely forward and gated on a binary antitrust outcome. Live: RIG $5.04 x 15.235 = $76.78 implied vs VAL $74.53 = $2.25 / 3.0% gross spread, exactly as stated. CORRECTIONS: the Australia ACCC review and the explicit '2H-2026 close' target are NOT in the primary VAL 8-K (the filing gives no close timeframe) — minor embellishments, not load-bearing. Modest 3% spread + binary regulatory gate justify score 7, not higher.
verified.analysis: Confirmed real, dated, forward arb: Transocean's signed Feb-9-2026 all-stock BCA (15.235 RIG/VAL, ~$5.8B) is pending after a May-4-2026 DOJ Second Request (and an added Australia ACCC review), pushing the targeted 2H-2026 close to execution risk. Live spread is genuine and unpriced for the antitrust outcome: 15.235 x $5.04 RIG = $76.78 implied vs VAL at $74.53 = ~$2.25 / 3.0% gross. Modest absolute spread plus a binary regulatory gate (no disclosed substantial-compliance date yet) is the only thing keeping this from a higher score; it clears Bloom G1/G2/G3 cleanly and is idiosyncratic, not a macro/oil bet.

verified.catalyst: China SAMR antitrust clearance — the sole remaining regulatory gate before the all-stock Axcelis/Veeco merger of equals (0.3575 ACLS per VECO) can close.
verified.dated_milestone: SAMR decision pending; merger outside date Sep 30 2026 (auto-extends if antitrust is the only open condition), targeted close 2H26.
verified.instrument: equity (VECO common; ACLS is the surviving acquirer)
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty Axcelis/SAMR) + G2 (concrete, shareholder-approved merger agreement with a fixed 0.3575 exchange ratio and a dated outside date)
verified.primary_source: ION Analytics Dealreporter "Veeco/Axcelis faces closer China antitrust scrutiny" (Mar 17 2026) + Axcelis/Veeco 8-K merger terms (0.3575 ratio); quotes VECO $69.47 / ACLS $176.13 (FMP, 2026-06-29).
verified.analysis: The merger is genuine, dated, and forward: shareholders of both companies approved Feb 6 2026, US HSR + Ireland/Germany/UK FDI are cleared, and China SAMR is the only open gate with a Sep 30 2026 outside date. But the triage thesis of a "clean arb" is wrong — VECO trades ~10% above its deal-implied value ($62.97), so there is no spread to capture; it is an all-stock merger of equals where VECO converts into 42% of the combined entity, meaning the position is effectively long ACLS with embedded China-antitrust binary risk (documented SAMR slowdown, sensitive-semis scrutiny, possible third-party complaints). Real catalyst, but not an unpriced edge and not a defined-payoff arb, so WATCH rather than ACTIVE.

verified.catalyst: FDA PDUFA decision on atacicept's accelerated-approval BLA for IgA nephropathy — first dual BAFF/APRIL B-cell modulator — due July 7, 2026.
verified.dated_milestone: PDUFA target action date July 7, 2026 (8 days out); separately, ORIGIN-3 eGFR full-approval analysis in Q3 2026 and sBLA filing Q4 2026.
verified.bloom_gates: G1+G2: G1 named counterparty = US FDA with a specific target action date; G2 concrete commitment = priority-review BLA accepted with mid-2026 commercial launch on track pending approval. (G3 partial — Breakthrough/priority status priced, but the binary approve/CRL outcome on a first-in-class mechanism is not fully discounted.)
verified.edge: M — hard dated binary with genuine optionality (year range $19–$56, price $43 near the 50-day avg, mcap ~$3.1B), but accelerated approval rests on proteinuria surrogate (46% reduction, ORIGIN), a relatively de-risked endpoint, and the event is well-telegraphed/well-covered, capping the unpriced edge.
verified.primary_source: Vera Therapeutics IR / Nasdaq press release "FDA Granted Priority Review to BLA for Atacicept for IgA Nephropathy" (announced Jan 7, 2026); corroborated by HCPLive and SEC 8-K data 0001831828 (FY2026). Price/mcap from FMP quote (latest session, ~$43.15).
verified.kill_fact: PRIMARY-corroborated: Jan 7, 2026 Vera press release (per SEC 8-K / StockTitan) — atacicept BLA "submitted using the Accelerated Approval Program, was assigned a PDUFA target action date of July 7, 2026" and accepted for Priority Review for adult IgAN. As of 2026-06-29 the decision is ~8 days forward = unfired binary. Live FMP quote VERA $43.15, mcap $3.097B, ~3.0M vol on NASDAQ — matches the line exactly. ORIGIN-3 interim showed 42% proteinuria reduction vs placebo (p<0.0001), NEJM Nov 6, 2025. The June 2, 2026 FDA-alignment news is the separate ORIGIN-3 eGFR/full-approval path (Q3 2026 analysis, sBLA Q4 2026, full approval 2027) and does NOT pre-empt the July 7 accelerated decision — correctly characterized in the line. No disqualifier found.
verified.analysis: Verified hard dated forward binary: FDA granted priority review to atacicept's accelerated-approval BLA for IgA nephropathy with a PDUFA target action date of July 7, 2026 (announced Jan 7, 2026), confirmed unfired with VERA at $43.15 (~$3.1B mcap) as of the latest quote. The catalyst is real, specific, dated, and forward — approval would make atacicept the first dual BAFF/APRIL B-cell modulator in IgAN with commercial launch on track for mid-2026. Note: the June 2, 2026 FDA-alignment news concerned the separate ORIGIN-3 eGFR analysis (Q3 2026) and sBLA full-approval path (Q4 2026 / approval 2027), which is a distinct downstream catalyst and does not pre-empt the July 7 accelerated-approval decision.

verified.catalyst: VinFast is splitting off and selling its Vietnam manufacturing arm (VFTP) to a founder-led purchaser group for ~$530M, moving ~$7B of debt off the listed VFS entity into an asset-light structure (VFVN retains IP/R&D/sales).
verified.dated_milestone: Deal close targeted Q3-2026 (next step: creditor approvals; shareholder approval already obtained at EGM 2026-05-27)
verified.instrument: equity (VFS, NASDAQ); low public float, founder-controlled
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty: Future Investment R&D JSC / "Tuong Lai," with CEO Pham Nhat Vuong as minority investor) + G3 (specific currently-quantified figure: ~$7B debt removed from VFS vs ~$530M proceeds)
verified.edge: L — primary equity gate (shareholder vote) already FIRED 2026-05-27 overwhelmingly; remaining creditor approval is procedural; terms fully public and stock has NOT re-rated up (near 52-wk low $2.78, below 50d/200d). Founder Vuong sits on both sides of the related-party debt transfer, so this is a controlled-shell deleveraging, not an arm's-length value unlock for minority equity.
verified.primary_source: SEC Form 6-K / Exhibit 99-1, "Announcement of the proposed corporate restructuring transaction" (sec.gov, filed ~May 2026); EGM results 6-K dated 2026-05-27; FMP quote 2026-06-29
verified.analysis: Catalyst is real, specific and dated (SEC 6-K; Q3-26 close), with a named founder-led counterparty and a concrete ~$7B debt-removal figure, satisfying G1+G3. But it is largely de-risked/partly fired: shareholders overwhelmingly approved the transfer at the May 27 2026 EGM, leaving only procedural creditor approval, and the stock has drifted to near its 52-week low rather than re-rating, signaling the market views the Vuong-on-both-sides related-party transfer skeptically rather than as a clean value unlock. Edge is low — it is a speculative founder-controlled equity lottery on a still-unprofitable EV maker, so it warrants WATCH-only monitoring through the Q3-26 close rather than active positioning.

verified.catalyst: ECLIPSE 1 Phase 3 chronic-hepatitis-delta topline (tobevibart + elebsiran) — a registrational, binary readout in a U.S.-unserved indication.
verified.dated_milestone: ECLIPSE 1 Phase 3 topline expected Q4 2026 (next date); ECLIPSE 2/3 Q1 2027; VIR-5818 HER2 Phase 1 data 2H 2026.
verified.bloom_gates: G3 partial — dated forward binary readout, but no contractual/unpriced figure (probabilistic clinical event, not a hard number). G1 (Astellas ~$1.7B deal) is a named counterparty but ALREADY FIRED Feb 2026, so it fails the forward test.
verified.edge: L-to-M — real dated catalyst but the biggest, most certain driver (Astellas) already fired and re-rated the stock ~133% YTD; remaining ECLIPSE 1 is binary with no payout floor, ~2 quarters out, soft 'expected' timing that could slip to Q1 2027; street target (~$21.56) already partially prices success and base case (~$11.5) sits at/below the ~$10.4 price.
verified.primary_source: Vir Biotechnology investor release "Upcoming 2026 Clinical Milestones," Jan 12 2026 (verbatim: "Topline data from the ECLIPSE 1 trial is expected in the fourth quarter of 2026") + FMP live quote $10.38, 52wk $4.16–$11.66, mktcap ~$1.75B (2026-06-29).
verified.analysis: VIR's nearest catalyst, the ECLIPSE 1 Phase 3 hep-delta topline, is genuine, dated (Q4 2026 per the company's own Jan-12-2026 milestone release), forward, and not-yet-fired — a registrational binary in an indication with no approved U.S. therapy, backed by clean 96-week Phase 2 SOLSTICE data (100% below LoQ). But it gates strictly to WATCH, not ACTIVE: it is a probabilistic clinical event with no contractual figure or payout floor (G3 only partially met), the largest and most certain catalyst — the ~$1.7B Astellas oncology deal (G1's named counterparty) — already fired in Feb 2026 and drove the ~133% YTD re-rate, so the cheap part of the asymmetry is spent, and at ~$10.4 the stock already sits at/above the ~$11.5 prob-weighted base case. Timing is also soft (~2 quarters out, "expected," with primary-completion reads hinting at possible Q1-27 slippage), and the upside to ~$20 is conditional on the data hitting while a miss carries it toward a ~$5.5 cash-plus-pipeline floor.

verified.catalyst: VK2735 (GLP-1/GIP) Phase 3 VANQUISH-1/2 obesity program fully enrolled, with a near-term Phase 1 maintenance-dosing readout and a pivotal topline still ahead.
verified.dated_milestone: Phase 1 maintenance-dosing study results expected 3Q26 (next dated event); pivotal VANQUISH-1/2 Phase 3 topline guided only to "2027" (no quarter), trials expected to complete in 2027.
verified.bloom_gates: G3 (specific dated clinical readout) — partial: the near-dated 3Q26 event is a low-impact maintenance-dosing study; the high-impact pivotal VANQUISH topline is binary but only loosely dated to 2027.
verified.edge: M — the 3Q26 maintenance readout is dated/forward but minor and unlikely to re-rate; the pivotal de-risking event (VANQUISH-1/2) is the real catalyst but undated ("2027"), binary, and faces a crowded, high-bar GLP-1 efficacy comparison vs Lilly/Novo, so asymmetry is muted at the index level.
verified.primary_source: Viking Therapeutics press release "Completion of Enrollment in Phase 3 VANQUISH-2 Trial of VK2735," dated 2026-03-26 (via StockTitan/PRNewswire); guidance: maintenance-dosing results 3Q26, VANQUISH studies to complete in 2027.
verified.analysis: VANQUISH-1 (n~4,650) enrolled Nov 2025 and VANQUISH-2 (n~1,000) enrolled Mar 2026, so the enrollment milestone has already fired; the next dated forward event is a Phase 1 maintenance-dosing readout guided to 3Q26, which is real and dated but a minor data point rather than a pivotal de-risking event. The pivotal VANQUISH topline — the event that actually matters for the binary thesis — is only guided to "2027" with no quarter, making it forward but not tightly dated, and it is a crowded GLP-1/GIP efficacy bet against an extremely high Lilly/Novo bar. Net: one genuine dated forward catalyst (3Q26 maintenance) plus an undated 2027 pivotal; supports WATCH, not ACTIVE.

verified.catalyst: FDA PDUFA decision on imsidolimab BLA for generalized pustular psoriasis (GPP), a rare-disease orphan indication, is a single dated forward binary regulatory event.
verified.dated_milestone: PDUFA target action date Dec 12, 2026 (BLA accepted, announced Feb 25, 2026)
verified.edge: M — real dated binary ~5.5mo out and stock sits near 52-wk low ($6.14 vs 52wk low $4.14, below 50/200d ~$6.41), so approval upside is not fully priced; but imsidolimab is a second-mover IL-36R inhibitor behind Boehringer's already-approved spesolimab/SPEVIGO in a small orphan GPP market, capping the unpriced magnitude.
verified.primary_source: Vanda Pharmaceuticals PR Newswire — FDA acceptance of imsidolimab BLA, target action date Dec 12, 2026 (Feb 25, 2026)
verified.analysis: Triage is correct: Bysanti (Feb 2026), NEREUS and tradipitant are already approved/fired and are not forward catalysts, while the imsidolimab GPP PDUFA on Dec 12, 2026 is the one genuine dated, forward, un-fired FDA binary. Confirmed against primary source (Vanda PR Newswire, announced Feb 25, 2026; corroborated HCPLive/StreetInsider) with pivotal data published in NEJM Evidence Apr 28, 2026. Gate passes on G3 (discrete, dated, not-yet-priced regulatory outcome), but it stays WATCH/score 5 rather than ACTIVE because it is a single second-mover orphan-indication approval (behind spesolimab) with bounded commercial upside, not a transformational catalyst.

verified.catalyst: Varonis is running an adviser-led exploration of a sale with preliminary takeover interest from Blackstone, Thoma Bravo and Vista, but no bid is signed.
verified.dated_milestone: Sale exploration disclosed Bloomberg Jun 23 2026; no fixed deadline — next read is a binding proposal / exclusivity headline or the Q2 FY26 print (early Aug 2026); deals at this stage typically resolve over 1-3 months.
verified.bloom_gates: G1 only — named counterparties (Blackstone/Thoma Bravo/Vista) confirmed; G2 fails (no binding commitment/bid); G3 fails (no offer price disclosed — the ~$4.7B cap is the re-rated trading value, not a take-out figure).
verified.edge: L — the leak has already re-rated the tape +23% (pre-news $32.71 → $40.21), the process is explicitly early-stage/preliminary with no signed LOI or exclusivity, and no offer price exists, so the unpriced spread to a plausible cyber take-out is now thin against material break risk.
verified.primary_source: Bloomberg via Bloomberg Law, "Cyber Firm Varonis Is Said to Weigh Sale After Takeover Interest," Jun 23 2026 (corroborated Investing.com / Yahoo Finance same date); current quote FMP $40.21 Jun 29 2026.
verified.analysis: The core triage claim is verified: Bloomberg (Jun 23 2026) confirmed Varonis is working with advisers and fielding preliminary interest from Blackstone, Thoma Bravo and Vista — a real, dated, named-counterparty cyber-security take-out setup, so Bloom G1 clears. However it largely fails the FORWARD/unpriced test: VRNS has already jumped from ~$32.71 pre-leak to $40.21 (+23%), far above its $30.25 50-day average, so the take-out optionality is substantially priced, not "NOT fired." With no signed bid (G2 fails), no disclosed offer price (G3 fails), and an explicitly early-stage process carrying high break risk, this is a WATCH, not an ACTIVE fired thesis — re-rate to ACTIVE only on a concrete bid/exclusivity headline.

verified.catalyst: FDA PDUFA decision on povetacicept BLA for IgA nephropathy (accelerated approval on proteinuria surrogate), Vertex's first nephrology product.
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty = FDA) + G3 (specific dated figure = Nov 30 2026 PDUFA on accepted BLA)
verified.edge: L — efficacy already de-risked/re-rated (Mar 9 2026 RAINIER Week 36 interim hit primary + all secondaries, 52% UPCR reduction p<0.0001, stock already moved); remaining event is a high-probability regulatory rubber-stamp on an already-accepted BLA via a well-precedented accelerated-approval pathway. Asymmetry thin: $1–2.5B peak-sales drug on a ~$127B mega-cap is ~1-2% of cap, not a needle-mover.
verified.primary_source: Vertex/BusinessWire press release "FDA Acceptance of BLA for Accelerated Approval of Povetacicept in IgA Nephropathy" (PDUFA Nov 30 2026), June 1, 2026; RAINIER Week 36 interim results, March 9, 2026
verified.analysis: The Nov 30, 2026 PDUFA is a genuine dated, forward binary with a named counterparty (FDA) on an accepted BLA, so it clears the literal gate. But the actual asymmetry is gone: the binary's hard part — efficacy — already fired on March 9, 2026 when the RAINIER Phase 3 Week 36 interim hit primary and all secondary endpoints (49.8% placebo-adjusted UPCR reduction), and the stock re-rated then; what remains is a high-probability approval on a well-trodden proteinuria-surrogate pathway. On a ~$127B cap, a $1–2.5B drug is pipeline diversification, not a mispriced event — triage was correct to flag it WATCH, and I'd score it slightly lower given the data catalyst has already passed.

verified.catalyst: Signed $31.00/share all-cash Paramount Skydance acquisition of WBD (shareholder-approved, DOJ-cleared unconditionally) trading at ~$27.13, a ~14% gross spread held wide by remaining EU/UK antitrust and state-AG tail risk before an expected Q3 2026 close.
verified.dated_milestone: EU Commission Phase-1 decision deadline July 7, 2026 (FSR foreign-subsidy decision by July 14); UK CMA refer/clear deadline Aug 7, 2026; ticking fee $0.25/qtr accrues daily if unclosed past Sept 30, 2026.

verified.edge: M — wide ~14% gross spread on a signed, shareholder-approved, DOJ-unconditionally-cleared all-cash deal is unusual; the discount is real compensation for EU FSR scrutiny of $24B Middle-East funding, a possible UK CMA Phase-2 referral, and threatened CA/NY state-AG litigation, so it is asymmetric but not riskless.
verified.primary_source: CBS News/Axios (DOJ clearance 2026-06-12); Variety/Deadline (EU 7/7 & 7/14, UK CMA 8/7, June 2026); FMP WBD $27.13 (2026-06-29)
verified.kill_fact: All load-bearing facts independently confirmed: WBD trades $27.13 (FMP live) vs $31.00 all-cash Paramount Skydance deal = ~$3.87/~14% gross spread; shareholder approval Apr 23 2026 (NBC/Variety/WBD); DOJ closed its investigation UNCONDITIONALLY (zero divestitures/remedies) June 12 2026 (Quiver/CBS/Concurrences); live forward gates are EU Phase-1 by July 7 + FSR provisional July 14 (Bloomberg/Variety) plus ~$24B Gulf sovereign funding (PIF ~$10B) driving the FSR/state-AG tail risk that holds the spread wide. CORRECTION: UK CMA is further along than the line implies — it had completed Phase 2 by ~June 9 entering a final phase due Aug 7, so a fresh five-month UK Phase-2 is somewhat overstated as a new threat (Aug 7 deadline still holds). Ticking fee ($0.25/qtr post-Sept 30) not independently verified but immaterial to thesis. Forward, dated, non-macro definitive-merger arb with a clear July 7 EU binary; tradeable liquid NASDAQ common. ACTIVE at moderated 7 justified.
verified.analysis: Triage note is materially stale: this is no longer a board "superior proposal" decision — WBD shareholders approved the $31.00 all-cash deal on April 23, 2026 and the DOJ cleared it unconditionally on June 12, 2026, so the only live gates are international (EU Phase-1 by July 7 plus FSR by July 14, UK CMA by Aug 7) and threatened US state-AG suits. At $27.13 the ~$3.87 (~14%) gross spread plus the $0.25/quarter ticking fee that begins accruing daily after Sept 30, 2026 forms a dated, forward, non-macro merger-arb with a clear near-term binary (the July 7 EU decision). The wide spread reflects genuine tail risk (Middle-East funding FSR review, a potential five-month UK Phase-2 that would jeopardize a Q3 close, and CA/NY litigation), which warrants ACTIVE at a moderated score rather than a higher conviction.

That residual is a thin annualized return for a cross-border cash-and-stock arb still exposed to Fed/ECB/DOJ approval and SAN ADS price risk, with a meaningful break-down to ~$66. G1/G2 pass but G3 fails (no unpriced figure), so this is a low-edge near-closed arb — WATCH, not ACTIVE.
